page: Microsoft Azure M128-64ms is an x86_64 memory-intensive server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8280L processor running at 2.7 GHz. Managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this configuration features 32 physical cores and 64 dedicated virtual CPUs with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 3892.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 60.81 GB per core. The instance includes 4194 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate a clear divergence between single-core and multi-threaded performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the weak and poor categories respectively. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression are rated poor. Conversely, multi-threaded operations show significant capability, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and large-block memory bandwidth, both read and write, scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M128-64ms is positioned as a highly specialized, resource-dense instance where the primary value lies in its massive memory capacity rather than raw single-core compute power. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Recommended applications include large-scale in-memory databases, heavy database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks that can leverage the strong large-block memory bandwidth and multi-threaded performance while tolerating lower single-core execution speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure M128-64ms is an x86_64 memory-intensive server instance configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8280L processor at 2.7 GHz. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 3892.0 GB of RAM, providing 60.81 GB of memory per core, alongside 4194 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, rank in the poor to weak categories, the instance delivers strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and large-block memory bandwidth. This hardware profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound enterprise applications rather than compute-heavy single-threaded tasks. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, complex database operations, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads that require massive RAM capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M128-64ms is a memory-intensive server featuring 64 vCPUs, 3892 GB of RAM, and 4194 GB of local storage, optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive x86_64 server featuring massive RAM capacity and strong multi-threaded memory bandwidth for large-scale database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical Intel Xeon 8280L cores
3892.0 GB of system memory providing 60.81 GB per core
4194 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-threaded decompression and large-block memory bandwidth
Suited for large-scale in-memory databases and memory-bound workloads
